objects.name: Analytic 1742
objects.description: The defender correlates managed-app runtime behavior indicative of command or shell invocation with subsequent spawned process or shell-like execution effects, then raises confidence when the resulting activity produces local artifacts or network communication outside expected user context. Because direct shell-process visibility can be weaker on iOS in many enterprise deployments, the analytic anchors first on process-creation or lower-level OS API effects where mobile telemetry can observe them, then on lifecycle context and post-execution network or file behavior. Confidence is strongest when the same app shows command invocation followed by process execution and immediate follow-on effects.

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: MobileEDR:telemetry
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: Managed app invokes lower-level OS process-launch or command-execution behavior before file or network effects, including interpreter-like execution flow where visible to sensor

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: MobileEDR:telemetry
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: Application spawns shell, command interpreter, or command-executing child process with arguments during command-execution phase

x_mitre_mutable_elements.description: Correlation window between command-execution indication, process effects, and follow-on file or network behavior
Managed apps legitimately expected to perform debugging, remote support, or enterprise automation tasks
Expected process-launch or helper-execution patterns for approved managed apps
Whether command-execution behavior should occur only during active user-driven workflows
Expected temporary or output file locations for approved app behavior
Minimum outbound volume after command execution to treat network behavior as meaningful
